message:
test(fcp-e2e): normalize google-docs manifest interface_hash via placeholder before recomputing

`docs_manifest_with_hash` reads the on-disk google-docs manifest,
recomputes its interface_hash, and rewrites the string to match. The
prior implementation did

    ConnectorManifest::parse_str_unchecked(raw) // unchecked
    let computed = manifest.compute_interface_hash();
    raw.replace(&manifest.manifest.interface_hash.to_string(), &computed)

which fails when `raw` contains any *other* substring that happens to
equal the pre-replace hash literal, and also fails when the
manifest's on-disk hash is a human-readable sentinel rather than a
valid blake3 digest (because `parse_str_unchecked` accepts anything
but `compute_interface_hash` re-canonicalizes the rest of the
manifest).

Switch to a two-step normalization:

1. Replace the human-readable hash
   `"blake3-256:fcp.interface.v2:google_docs_v1"` with an all-zero
   blake3 placeholder
   `"blake3-256:fcp.interface.v2:0000...0000"` (64 hex zeros).
2. Parse the normalized text via `parse_str_unchecked`, compute the
   real `interface_hash`, then replace the placeholder with the
   computed hex digest.

This isolates the substring we rewrite (the placeholder appears
exactly once in the manifest), avoids rewriting any unrelated hash
value that might be present elsewhere in the TOML, and keeps the
input to `compute_interface_hash` well-formed so the computation is
deterministic across runs.

No production code is touched; the test's invocation path and
downstream assertions are unchanged.
